title: Multi-agent roles: planner/implementer/reviewer generalized from research_mode
current_state: research_mode is a working 3-model debate but hardcoded to its one shape (question → 2 rounds → synthesis). Phase 10 wants role-based internal collaboration (researcher fetches, coder drafts, reviewer critiques) and there is no abstraction for it.
target_state: core/roles.py: a Role = (name, router_tier, system_framing, allowed_tools) — deliberately ModeConfig-shaped. A RolePipeline runs roles in sequence over a shared scratchpad dict (plan → draft → critique → final). First concrete pipeline: 'careful answer' (cheap-planner → default-drafter → cheap-critic) invoked by an explicit 'deliberate:' prefix or by T-191's escalation heuristic. research_mode reimplemented as a RolePipeline (proof the abstraction holds), old entry point kept.
migration_plan: 1. core/roles.py with Role + RolePipeline (sequential first — parallel debate later; YAGNI).
2. Scratchpad = plain dict passed role-to-role, capped size, dumped to logs/roles/ per run for inspection.
3. Port research_mode onto RolePipeline behind its existing interface (tests must pass unchanged).
4. 'deliberate:' prefix in _process_input_inner routes through the careful-answer pipeline.
5. Tests: pipeline ordering, scratchpad flow, research_mode parity, cost attribution per role in evolution log.
risk_notes: Scope trap: do NOT build agent-to-agent chat protocols. Sequential pipeline over a dict is enough to ship value; revisit parallelism only with evidence.
